steps.title: Identify the subject and its number
steps.content: The subject is "Alexander's use of 'we'" — the head noun is "use" (singular). The intervening prepositional phrase "of 'we'" does not change the subject's number, so the verb must be singular.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement and eliminate the others
steps.content:
Subject-verb agreement (rule: a singular subject takes a singular verb) requires the singular present-tense form, which is "evokes."
  • Choice A — evokes: singular present-tense verb agreeing with the singular head noun "use." ✓
  • Choice B — are evoking: plural present progressive; disagrees with the singular subject. ✗
  • Choice C — have evoked: plural present perfect; the singular form would be "has evoked," so this also fails agreement. ✗
  • Choice D — evoke: plural present tense; disagrees with the singular subject. ✗
